  "findings": [
    "Caliper guide pins are dry and seized, preventing proper floating action of the caliper.",
    "Uneven brake pad wear and reduced braking efficiency will occur if not corrected."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove the caliper guide pins.",
    "Clean or replace the guide pins and slide bores.",
    "Lubricate the pins with high-temperature silicone brake grease before reassembly."
  ],
  "rationale": "OEM specifications and standard automotive shop practices require caliper guide pins to be clean, free-moving, and properly lubricated to ensure even pad wear and prevent caliper binding. Seized pins constitute a critical installation defect.",
  "references": [
    "OEM Brake Service Specifications",
    "FMVSS 135 (Light Vehicle Brake Systems) performance standards"
  ]
}
